tagline: Multi-mode AI security auditor: STRIDE/PASTA threat modeling, red/blue team, hardening, scoring and incident-response playbooks for any.
verdict: Well-structured 6-phase audit process with a defined scoring rubric and output contract. Undermined by hardcoded author-only Windows script paths that break for every other user, suspicious 'security-allowlist' HTML comments next to exec/eval mentions, duplicated bulk playbook content, and no declared tooling or versioning.

highlights.summary: HTML comment '<!-- security-allowlist: defensive audit trigger -->' next to eval/exec/os.system mentions reads as an instruction to a scanner to allow-list the line.
Same 'security-allowlist' HTML comments (lines 207, 386) are non-rendering machine-directed tags embedded next to risk-relevant text, not human TODOs.
Ten+ python script invocations (e.g. score_calculator.py) with no mention anywhere of missing-tool, script-not-found, or execution-failure handling.
